headline: Day detail pages now show the full technical specification inline
summary: Each day's page now shows the full technical specification right on the page, so you can read exactly what was planned without downloading any files.

candidates.title: Inline Spec Viewer on Day Detail Pages
candidates.summary: Add a collapsible 'Technical Specification' section to the day detail page so visitors can read the full spec without leaving the page or downloading files.

keep: Every future day automatically gets spec viewing — a permanent improvement
Collapsible details element keeps the page scannable for casual visitors
mustChange: Long specs need a max-height with overflow scroll to prevent layout disruption
risks: If spec.md is missing, the section should display a clear empty-state message

keep: Fills a genuine gap — specs were referenced but not viewable
Step 6 placement makes sense in the pipeline narrative
mustChange: Markdown rendering must handle code blocks and tables gracefully
risks: Very long specs could overwhelm the page layout on mobile

keep: Completes the decision trail from scoring → planning → execution
Uses existing renderMarkdown utility — minimal new code
mustChange: Empty-state message needed when spec.md is not available
risks: Spec content could contain unexpected markdown that breaks rendering

keep: Compounding improvement — every future day benefits automatically
Completes the full decision trail visibility
mustChange: Max-height with overflow scroll for long specs
Clear empty-state message when spec is unavailable
risks: Very long specs could overwhelm mobile layout
Unexpected markdown content could break rendering
feedbackReferences: Visitors wanted to see the full spec without downloading files
The day detail page was missing the planning step between judging and building

candidates.title: Day Comparison View
candidates.summary: Add a side-by-side comparison mode to see how two different days' decisions and outcomes differed.
keep: Interesting concept for showing evolution over time
mustChange: Too complex for current stage — needs routing, state management, and responsive side-by-side layout
risks: Only 3 days of content makes comparison less meaningful
Side-by-side layout is challenging on mobile

candidates.title: Artifact Download Bundle
candidates.summary: Add a one-click download button that packages all of a day's artifacts into a ZIP file for offline reading.
keep: Useful for developers who want to study the pipeline output
mustChange: Requires client-side ZIP generation library — adds dependency weight
risks: Low usage — most visitors won't download artifacts
ZIP generation in browser has compatibility concerns

winner.title: Inline Spec Viewer on Day Detail Pages
winner.summary: A new collapsible section on every day detail page renders the technical specification inline, so visitors can read the full spec without downloading anything.

rationale: The inline spec viewer scored highest because it fills a genuine gap in the day detail page — specs were referenced by the pipeline but never viewable on the page itself. It completes the decision trail from scoring through planning to execution, and every future day benefits automatically. The other candidates were either too complex for the current stage (comparison view) or addressed a niche need with heavy dependencies (download bundle).

feedbackInfluence.summary: Visitors wanted to see the full technical spec without leaving the page or downloading files.
The day detail page was missing the planning step between the judging and building sections.

bluesky_post.headline: Every day's technical spec is now readable right on the page
bluesky_post.body: We added a collapsible Technical Specification section to every day detail page — view the full spec without downloading anything. The complete decision trail is now visible. #CommandGarden #BuildInPublic
bluesky_post.alt_text: Screenshot of the Command Garden day detail page showing a collapsible Technical Specification section between the feedback and build summary steps.

replyTemplates.whatIs: Command Garden is a site that builds itself — AI agents propose, score, and ship one change per day, with every decision published transparently.
replyTemplates.howDoesItWork: Three AI judges score candidate changes across 7 dimensions. The winner gets spec'd, built, tested, and published — all in one cycle. Today we shipped inline spec viewing.
replyTemplates.genericQuestion: Great question! Command Garden publishes every decision artifact — scores, specs, build logs — so you can see exactly how autonomous AI builds software.
